description: A process or system is 'poruli' when it produces much useful output in relation to a selected input. The boundary matters: a stove may use little fuel but demand many hours of labour. It is efficient by one measure and not by the other. 'poru' (productive) reports useful results; 'poruli' compares them with what was spent.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into true-mid rounded back 'o'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r' and move into stressed high rounded back 'u'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: 'poruli' spends one complete closure on opening 'po'. The stressed tap or short trill and final lateral then carry the word without another stop, an economical contour that can help the meaning stay in memory.
usage_notes: 'poruli' modifies a following noun; when predicative, it precedes 'nai' (be). Its noun form means efficiency. An informative claim gives the input and useful output, then leaves its boundary open to inspection. Sustainability and goodness are separate judgements.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A repair may save material while consuming scarce labour. Naming both sides keeps an efficiency claim useful without letting one favourable measure do all the talking.

semantic_domains.activity: a relation between resources used and useful performance
semantic_domains.cognition: an assessment whose result depends on selected inputs, outputs, and boundaries
semantic_domains.creation: an efficiency judgement applied to a designed process or system
